acceptance: A review-labeled bead closed with an explicit `--commit` or with real staged implementation/docs changes records the exact closing_commit_sha unless the close is metadata-only, and regression coverage proves metadata-only review closes still omit the field while non-metadata review closes preserve it.
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: cli/cmd/bead.go:175
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: Commit 91f17a05b054b92d6d2b883bb4563e18fc2ce4d8 treats every bead carrying review labels as metadata-only by setting `recordClosingProvenance := !isMetadataOnlyReviewBead(beadBeforeClose)` and defining `isMetadataOnlyReviewBead` purely from labels. That is broader than the fix described in the commit message and FEAT-019: only metadata-only review closes should omit `closing_commit_sha`. Review beads can legitimately govern real docs or code changes, and this patch now drops their provenance even when the caller provides `--commit` or the close lands alongside real staged work. In a throwaway git repo with the current CLI binary, closing a normal bead with `--commit <short-head>` recorded `closing_commit_sha`, while closing an otherwise identical `action:review` bead with the same explicit commit omitted the field entirely. That makes replay and audit for non-metadata review passes fall back to sessionless/manual mode despite an exact implementation boundary being available.
Suggested fix: Narrow the suppression check to actual metadata-only review closes instead of all review-labeled beads, and add regression coverage for both explicit-commit and mixed-close review flows so non-metadata review beads still retain exact closing provenance.
title: correctness: preserve closing provenance for non-metadata review beads

acceptance: FEAT-002 explicitly defines the canonical project-scoped route family for document, bead, doc-graph, agent-session, execution, and mutation surfaces, plus the matching project-selection rule for project-aware MCP tools; and any remaining unscoped /api/... or /mcp/... forms are documented only as singleton compatibility aliases rather than the primary contract.
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-002-server.md:65
Category: correctness
Severity: high
Description: The commit adds the multi-project registry and says unscoped `/api/...` and `/mcp/...` forms are only compatibility aliases, but the numbered FEAT-002 requirements for documents, beads, doc graph, agent activity, executions, and bead mutations still specify only the legacy unscoped endpoints and tools. That leaves the governing feature contract internally inconsistent: an implementation can satisfy the numbered requirements without ever exposing the canonical `/api/projects/:project/...` resource families or project-scoped MCP semantics promised by SD-019 and by bead ddx-efd858c4 acceptance.
Suggested fix: Thread project-scoped routing through the FEAT-002 functional requirements for every affected surface, or add an explicit default rule that all server resource routes and project-aware MCP tools are scoped by `/api/projects/:project/...` and an equivalent project selector unless they are registry-wide. Keep legacy unscoped forms documented only as singleton compatibility aliases.
title: correctness: make FEAT-002 resource routes explicitly project-scoped

acceptance: The frontend CI job rebuilds cli/internal/server/frontend/dist before compiling cli/build/ddx, and a change limited to frontend src cannot pass Playwright by reusing stale committed dist assets
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: .github/workflows/ci.yml:163
Category: integration
Severity: medium
Description: The new frontend-e2e job compiles the Go server with `make build` before any frontend bundle build step, and the server embeds checked-in files from `cli/internal/server/frontend/dist` via `//go:embed all:frontend/dist`. FEAT-008 defines the build pipeline as `bun run build` producing `dist/` before `go build`, so this job can pass Playwright against a stale committed bundle instead of the current `src/` when frontend code changes without regenerating dist. That creates a false-negative CI path for the very regressions this job is supposed to catch.
Suggested fix: Install frontend dependencies with the declared frontend toolchain, run the frontend build to refresh `cli/internal/server/frontend/dist`, then compile the Go binary so Playwright exercises the current embedded UI assets.
title: integration: rebuild frontend dist before Playwright CI run

acceptance: API-001 and FEAT-006 agree on a single owner for required post-run checks and land/preserve semantics; no implementation path requires running those steps twice around one execute-bead attempt
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: docs/helix/02-design/contracts/API-001-execute-bead-supervisor-contract.md:65
Category: correctness
Severity: high
Description: API-001 says the supervisor should run `ddx agent execute-bead` and then separately run required post-run checks, classify the outcome, and apply land/preserve semantics. FEAT-006 already defines `ddx agent execute-bead` itself as the canonical workflow that resolves execution documents, runs required executions, evaluates merge eligibility, and either lands or preserves the iteration. Implementing API-001 as written would either duplicate required executions and landing logic around `execute-bead` or force FEAT-006 to become stale without saying so, which makes the command/supervisor boundary ambiguous for the first shipped worker.
Suggested fix: Make one layer authoritative. Either define the supervisor as a thin wrapper around `ddx agent execute-bead` result states, or narrow `execute-bead` into a lower-level primitive and update FEAT-006/SD-012 accordingly so post-run checks and land/preserve ownership live in exactly one place.
title: correctness: avoid duplicating execute-bead post-run and landing ownership in API-001

acceptance: Given a bead whose first close and only correlated sessions are before the cutoff but whose updated_at is later, metrics summary --since excludes it from bead totals and cost/feature unknown counts; a regression test covers the late-update/no-facts case; cd cli && go test ./internal/processmetrics -count=1 passes
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: cli/internal/processmetrics/processmetrics.go:285
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: The new summary window helper uses bead `updated_at` as the inclusion gate, so a bead whose close fact and only correlated sessions are both before the cutoff still appears in `ddx metrics summary --since ...` if it was edited later for unrelated reasons. `beadsVisibleInSummaryWindow` admits that bead, and the downstream summary logic counts it in `summary.Beads.Total/Closed`, `summary.Beads.UnknownCost`, `summary.Cost.Beads`, and `summary.Cost.Features` even though there are no in-window lifecycle or session facts. Repro: with one bead closed on 2026-01-01, one correlated session on 2026-01-01, and only `updated_at=2026-03-05`, `Summary(Query{Since: 2026-02-01, HasSince: true})` returns `beads.total=1 closed=1 unknown_cost=1 cost.beads=1` while sessions, cycle-time, and rework are all zero. SD-016 says `--since` means include only facts observed on or after the cutoff for the relevant fact type; a tracker metadata edit after the window is not a new lifecycle or cost fact.
Suggested fix: Define summary bead inclusion from the same fact timelines used by the underlying sections, rather than raw `updated_at`. A bead with no in-window close/reopen/session facts should not surface in the windowed summary or its cost/feature counts, and regression coverage should include the late-update/no-facts case.
title: correctness: summary since window must ignore late-updated pre-cutoff beads

acceptance: A focused agent config regression test proves unsupported preset names from .ddx/config.yaml and AGENT_PRESET do not silently flow into prompt.NewFromPreset; invalid values are either normalized intentionally or surfaced via an explicit warning/error path rather than relying on library fallback.
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:190
Category: integration
Severity: medium
Description: Commit 7b06cfca changes the built-in default preset to agent, but resolveAgentConfig still copies any .ddx/config.yaml preset and AGENT_PRESET value straight into cfg.Preset at lines 190-215 and later passes it to prompt.NewFromPreset. In github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/agent v0.1.0, prompt.GetPreset falls back to agent for unknown names, so stale values like forge are still silently accepted. That means the runtime continues to rely on unknown-name fallback for user-supplied config and env values, so hx-96df1675's acceptance criterion about eliminating active config/runtime fallback paths is still not met.
Suggested fix: Validate configured preset names against the embedded agent library's supported preset set when loading config and env, then either normalize intentionally supported aliases or surface a deterministic warning/error for unsupported values, with regression coverage for invalid configured presets.
title: integration: validate configured agent presets instead of silently falling back

acceptance: A regression test covers a harness with an earlier routing-outcomes.jsonl row and a later legacy-only sessions.jsonl row lacking any matching native_session_id, trace_id, or span_id, and ddx agent usage reports both sessions without double-counting mirrored rows; cd cli && go test ./cmd -run TestAgentUsage -count=1 passes.
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: cli/cmd/agent_usage.go:208
Category: integration
Severity: medium
Description: Commit 9f96a3e scopes the legacy-session cutoff by harness, but `aggregateUsageAggs` still drops every `sessions.jsonl` row at or after the first routing outcome timestamp for that harness whether or not that session actually has a mirrored routing outcome (`cutoffByHarness` at lines 208-210 and 256-279). That remains an undercount path because session logging and routing-outcome logging are separate best-effort writes: the runner always appends the session first and then ignores any `AppendOutcome` failure (`cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:153-163`, `cli/internal/agent/routing_metrics.go:214-256`). If one later routing-outcome append is skipped or fails, `ddx agent usage` silently drops the valid legacy session row for that same harness as soon as any earlier outcome exists.
Suggested fix: Gate legacy-row suppression on actual mirrored identity matches rather than a blanket per-harness timestamp cutoff, or persist an explicit per-row migration marker that proves the session was mirrored before dropping it; add regression coverage for a harness with an earlier routing outcome plus a later legacy-only session row that lacks matching native/trace/span identifiers.
title: integration: keep same-harness legacy sessions visible when routing outcomes are missing

description:

acceptance: Given mixed pre-cutoff and post-cutoff bead/session facts, metrics summary with --since excludes pre-cutoff totals and summary counts/aggregates match the chosen window semantics consistently across bead, session, cost, cycle-time, and rework sections; regression tests cover the filtered summary case
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: cli/internal/processmetrics/processmetrics.go:265
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: `Summary` accepts a windowed query but still counts all beads and all sessions from `loadInputs()` before applying the cutoff, so `--since` produces internally inconsistent dashboards. In `Summary`, `summary.Beads.Total/Open/Closed` and `summary.Sessions.*` are accumulated from the full stores (`len(beads)` and the unfiltered `sessions` loop), while cycle-time and rework use the cutoff and cost partially windows only per-session math. Repro: from `cli/`, a temporary fixture with one January bead/session and one March bead/session and `since=2026-02-01` prints `beads_total=2 sessions_total=2 cost_beads=2 cycle_known=1`, so pre-cutoff facts still appear in the top-level summary even though only one bead is inside the requested window.
Suggested fix: Define summary window semantics from SD-016's windowing rules and apply them consistently across bead, session, cost, cycle-time, and rework aggregates so a `--since` summary only reports facts observed on or after the cutoff.
title: correctness: honor --since across metrics summary aggregates

acceptance: A config containing agent.agent_runner.preset: forge fails config validation before runtime resolution, and regression coverage proves the schema's allowed preset set stays aligned with prompt.PresetNames()
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: cli/internal/config/schema/config.schema.json:276
Category: integration
Severity: medium
Description: Commit 3b26aac39659d65ea161498720609c15b2607726 updates the embedded config schema's default, description, and examples for agent_runner.preset, and adds a regression test that checks only those metadata fields. But the schema entry at cli/internal/config/schema/config.schema.json:276-283 still declares preset as a plain string with no enum, while ConfigLoader treats this embedded schema as the user-facing validation boundary in cli/internal/config/loader.go:68-70. As a result, .ddx/config.yaml values like preset: forge still pass config validation and only fail later when resolveAgentConfig checks prompt.PresetNames(), so the schema/validator surface remains out of sync with the actual supported preset set even after this fix.
Suggested fix: Add schema-level allowed-value enforcement for agent_runner.preset, ideally from the same supported preset source used by runtime validation, and add a focused config-loader or validator regression test proving unsupported preset values are rejected during config validation rather than at agent execution time.
title: integration: enforce supported agent_runner presets in config schema
Config-only close

Description: Commit 4d247a6bb67e122181a1cc0f005e6edcd83afee4 closes review-finding bead hx-f2b93e92 by recording closing_commit_sha=1bc26181bae1e88cd6c29fc11f20c5904ed2ae8a. As recorded now, hx-f2b93e92 replays against unrelated bead-close code instead of representing a metadata-only repair finding.
Suggested fix: Clear hx-f2b93e92.closing_commit_sha and leave the bead closed without replay provenance, or otherwise record only the actual governed implementation boundary if a non-metadata repair commit exists and is documented.
title: correctness: clear misleading closing provenance on hx-f2b93e92

acceptance: In a non-git working directory, a review bead that already has closing_commit_sha=1234567890abcdef1234567890abcdef12345678 keeps that exact value after 'ddx bead close <id>'; metadata-only review closes in git repos still clear only positively identified '.ddx/beads.jsonl'-only backfill SHAs; and bead close acceptance tests cover both the non-git preserve path and the tracker-only clear path.
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: cli/cmd/bead.go:150
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: Commit 93ac319 narrows metadata-only review-close cleanup by checking whether the existing `closing_commit_sha` points at a tracker-only backfill before deleting it, but `commitIsMetadataOnlyTrackerBackfill` treats any `git show` failure as metadata-only (`return true`). Reproduced against the checked-out source by building `/tmp/ddx-review` from `cli/`, running it in a temp directory with `DDX_BEAD_DIR` and no git repo, creating a review bead, setting `closing_commit_sha=1234567890abcdef1234567890abcdef12345678`, and then closing the bead. The bead closes successfully but the stored `closing_commit_sha` disappears. That regresses FEAT-019 replay/audit semantics for portable/manual review workflows: when git metadata is unavailable, a valid previously recorded implementation boundary is erased even though the command cannot prove it was a tracker-only backfill.
Suggested fix: Make `commitIsMetadataOnlyTrackerBackfill` fail open for provenance preservation when commit inspection is unavailable, or otherwise only clear an existing `closing_commit_sha` after positively proving the referenced commit is a `.ddx/beads.jsonl`-only backfill; add regression coverage for closing a review bead with a pre-existing full SHA in a non-git workspace.
title: correctness: preserve existing review provenance when git lookup is unavailable

acceptance: A session row with cost_usd=0 is reported with cost_state=known, contributes to known-cost counts instead of estimated-cost counts, the -1 sentinel still maps to unknown, and regression tests cover zero-cost + sentinel cases
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: cli/internal/processmetrics/processmetrics.go:1083
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: `sessionCostState` and `sessionDerivedCost` treat any non-positive `cost_usd` as not-known by checking `sess.CostUSD > 0`. SD-016 explicitly says known-zero values are valid and must remain `cost_state=known`; only the `-1` sentinel means unknown. Repro: from `cli/`, a temporary fixture with one correlated session using model `qwen/qwen3-coder-30b` and `cost_usd=0` prints `state=estimated cost=0` from `processmetrics.New(dir).Cost(...)` instead of `state=known cost=0`.
Suggested fix: Treat `cost_usd >= 0` as known, handle the `-1` sentinel explicitly, and add regression coverage for zero-cost local sessions in both cost rows and summary counters.
title: correctness: classify zero-cost sessions as known in process metrics
Review issue

acceptance: Given a local plugin manifest sets install.root.target under `~/`, when `ddx install --local` runs inside a project, then both the global installed root and `.ddx/plugins/<name>` exist and project-relative plugin resource paths resolve
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: cli/cmd/install.go:244
Category: integration
Severity: medium
Description: Registry installs explicitly create `.ddx/plugins/<name>` as a project-local symlink when the manifest root target is global (`~/...`) so project-relative plugin paths still resolve. The new manifest-driven local install path does not mirror that behavior: it creates the global symlink only. Repro: a local plugin whose manifest sets `install.root.target: ~/.ddx/plugins/sample-plugin` produces `global_plugin_exists=yes`, `project_plugin_exists=no`, and still installs skills. Any code or docs that resolve plugin resources through `.ddx/plugins/<name>/...` now break in local developer installs.
Suggested fix: When `installLocal` honors a global root target, also create the same project-local `.ddx/plugins/<name>` symlink that `InstallPackage` creates for registry installs.
title: integration: keep project-local plugin path for local installs with global roots

acceptance: SD-015 defines a stable model-level attribution key for routing metrics so RoutingOutcome, QuotaSnapshot, and any derived burn/performance summaries can be associated with the resolved candidate model or canonical target; FEAT-014 stays consistent with that key; and the docs no longer imply that latency/cost/reliability from different models on the same surface are mixed into one routing signal
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-015-agent-routing-and-catalog-resolution.md:175
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: The new minimal-routing schema records `RoutingOutcome`, `QuotaSnapshot`, and `BurnSummary` only by `harness` and `surface`. Earlier in the same design, however, each `CandidatePlan` carries `CanonicalTarget`, `ConcreteModel`, `EstimatedCostUSD`, and `PerformanceMetrics` because routing decisions can differ materially by resolved model or exact pin. A single surface can host very different models with different latency, reliability, and cost characteristics. If DDx aggregates outcome and burn data only at the harness/surface level, runs for one model will contaminate routing signals for another, making model-specific scoring and exact-pin behavior incorrect.
Suggested fix: Extend the minimal routing-metrics schema with a model-identifying dimension such as `canonical_target` or `concrete_model` (or explicitly define an equivalent normalization key), and update FEAT-014/SD-015 so performance, cost, and burn inputs remain attributable to the resolved model rather than only the surface.
title: correctness: keep routing metrics model-specific

acceptance: With git.auto_commit=always, auto-committing a nested relative path succeeds without a git pathspec error, and a regression test covers a relative-path caller such as ddx doc stamp or AutoCommit itself
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: cli/internal/git/autocommit.go:65
Category: integration
Severity: medium
Description: AutoCommit now changes the git working directory to filepath.Dir(filePath) but still runs git add with the original filePath. That works for absolute paths like .ddx/beads.jsonl in bead close, but it breaks existing relative nested-path callers. For example, from /Users/erik/Projects/ddx, running `cd cli && git add -n cli/cmd/bead.go` fails with `fatal: pathspec 'cli/cmd/bead.go' did not match any files`, because the path is interpreted relative to the new cwd as cli/cli/cmd/bead.go. cli/cmd/doc.go passes relative document paths into AutoCommit, so doc stamping auto-commit will now fail for nested docs paths.\nSuggested fix: Normalize filePath before staging when AutoCommit changes directories. Either resolve filePath to an absolute path and convert it to a repo-root-relative path for `git add`, or keep the cwd at the repo root and only change it for repo detection/rev-parse.
title: integration: preserve relative-path auto-commit callers

acceptance: ddx bead show ddx-1210706d --json no longer reports closing_commit_sha=9653820049db7edebe0374431544b1b8a8dbae88, and the provenance field used by FEAT-019 replay is documented or tested to identify the original governed-work diff rather than a later metadata-only tracker commit
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: .ddx/beads.jsonl:1
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: Commit 34b87b9 repoints ddx-1210706d.closing_commit_sha to 9653820049db7edebe0374431544b1b8a8dbae88 because that SHA is a pure tracker-only commit. But FEAT-019 replay uses closing_commit_sha as the original commit boundary: it checks out the parent of that SHA and compares against that commit's diff. For ddx-1210706d, that means replay now uses e51936a as the base state but compares against 9653820's one-line metadata diff, so the replay/audit surface no longer represents the actual tracker-governance work that bead governed, including the concerns.md taxonomy change mentioned in the bead acceptance. The last-commit fix removes the mixed-commit problem but replaces it with a metadata-only provenance target that cannot reconstruct the original governed-work diff.
Suggested fix: Either clear ddx-1210706d.closing_commit_sha so replay falls back to sessionless/manual semantics for this backfilled bead, or introduce an explicitly documented and tested provenance model that distinguishes implementation diff provenance from later tracker-close metadata commits before storing a pure metadata SHA in closing_commit_sha.
title: correctness: stop ddx-1210706d replay provenance from pointing at metadata-only commit

acceptance: `cd cli && DDX_BEAD_DIR=$(mktemp -d)/.ddx go test ./cmd/... -run TestBeadCommandsUnset.*` passes, and `ddx bead update <id> --unset events` either fails or leaves `ddx bead evidence list <id> --json` unchanged for a bead with existing evidence
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: cli/cmd/bead.go:373
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: The new generic `ddx bead update --unset` path deletes any key from `b.Extra`, but append-only execution evidence is also stored in `b.Extra["events"]`. In a temp bead store, `go run . bead evidence add <id> ...` followed by `go run . bead update <id> --unset events` makes `ddx bead evidence list <id> --json` return `[]`, so this patch gives normal metadata updates a data-loss path that can erase audit evidence and violate the append-only contract of `ddx bead evidence`.
Suggested fix: Restrict `--unset` to an allowlist of removable provenance fields such as `closing_commit_sha`, or explicitly reject protected keys like `events`/`session_id` and add a regression test proving evidence cannot be removed through `bead update`.
title: correctness: prevent bead update --unset from erasing evidence

acceptance: A supported `ddx bead` workflow exists to repair closing_commit_sha on closed beads, and provenance backfills like ddx-5751d1a2 no longer require direct .ddx/beads.jsonl row edits.
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: .ddx/beads.jsonl:5
Category: drift
Severity: medium
Description: Commit 21e90117937e19ea62579328f27fd608714ccc7f repairs ddx-5751d1a2 by directly rewriting the managed row in .ddx/beads.jsonl. AGENTS.md explicitly forbids manual edits to bead storage and requires agents to use `ddx bead` subcommands or stop and ask if the CLI cannot perform the operation. FEAT-004 and SD-004 also define tracker mutations around DDx-controlled snapshot writes and atomic rename semantics. Landing this provenance repair as a hand-edited JSONL row bypasses the supported mutation path and normalizes exactly the storage-edit workflow the repo policy is trying to prevent.
Suggested fix: Add or use a supported `ddx bead` command path to repair `closing_commit_sha` on closed beads, then reapply this provenance repair through that command flow instead of a manual .ddx/beads.jsonl edit.
title: drift: stop hand-editing DDx-managed bead storage for provenance repairs

acceptance: SD-016 states that cost_usd=-1 means unknown rather than known cost, direct cost aggregation excludes the sentinel value, and the cost_state / cost precedence rules align with FEAT-014 and FEAT-006 sentinel semantics
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-016-process-metrics.md:191
Category: correctness
Severity: high
Description: SD-016 defines `cost_state=known` whenever a session row reports `cost_usd` and says bead lifecycle cost should use `session.cost_usd` whenever it is present. But FEAT-014 and FEAT-006 explicitly define `cost_usd=-1` as the sentinel for unknown cost, not a real price. Implementing SD-016 as written would treat unknown-cost sessions as known values, producing negative dollar totals and skipping the estimation/unknown fallback for a core FEAT-016 metric.
Suggested fix: Update SD-016 so `cost_usd=-1` is mapped to `cost_state=unknown` and excluded from direct cost sums, with estimation or unknown fallback applied only to non-sentinel values.
title: correctness: treat cost_usd=-1 as unknown in SD-016

acceptance: ddx bead show hx-010a50cf --json omits closing_commit_sha, and closing a metadata-only review bead through the supported ddx CLI no longer stamps the prior HEAD or unrelated metadata commit as closing provenance when no new close commit lands
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: .ddx/beads.jsonl:27
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: During this last-commit review, the supported `ddx bead close hx-010a50cf` flow closed a metadata-only `action:review` bead and persisted `closing_commit_sha=d20a24fd8ff635481f19c934e8643472ed0a7cde` on the new tracker row. That SHA is the previously reviewed provenance-repair commit, not a close commit created by the review workflow, and FEAT-019 explicitly says tracker-only backfills should omit `closing_commit_sha` rather than point replay at an unrelated metadata or earlier implementation boundary. The repo source and tests still indicate review beads should suppress this field, so the durable tracker state produced by the current supported CLI path remains misleading.
Suggested fix: Make the supported bead-close path omit `closing_commit_sha` for metadata-only review closes when no landed close commit exists, add regression coverage for that no-new-commit path, and repair hx-010a50cf so its closing provenance is absent rather than pointing at d20a24f.
title: correctness: metadata-only review close still stamps prior HEAD provenance

acceptance: With git.auto_commit=always in a git repo and DDX_BEAD_DIR unset, running 'ddx bead close <id>' leaves 'git status --short' clean, and 'git show HEAD -- .ddx/beads.jsonl' includes the bead row with closing_commit_sha equal to 'git rev-parse HEAD' rather than requiring an extra uncommitted worktree update
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: cli/cmd/bead.go:518
Category: integration
Severity: medium
Description: The new close flow writes bead status first, auto-commits .ddx/beads.jsonl, and only then writes closing_commit_sha back to the store. In a clean temp git repo with git.auto_commit=always and DDX_BEAD_DIR unset, running 'ddx bead close <id>' leaves 'git status --short' dirty with ' M .ddx/beads.jsonl'. The landed HEAD commit 9494afc82408cac9a650d616fc3207197e1dfbd6 contains only the status=open->closed change, while the durable closing_commit_sha appears only in the uncommitted worktree diff. That means the command still fails to land close provenance atomically and leaves replay/audit metadata outside the recorded close commit.
Suggested fix: Restructure bead close so the recorded closing_commit_sha is present in the same landed commit that closes the bead, or perform and persist a second deterministic commit/update so git status is clean after close and HEAD contains the final provenance-bearing row.
title: integration: persist close provenance in the landed tracker commit

acceptance: Given installed.yaml contains both plugin and resource entries, when `ddx doctor --plugins` runs, then it emits plugin_validation findings only for plugin-like entries and no false `package.yaml` findings for resources
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: cli/cmd/doctor.go:452
Category: integration
Severity: high
Description: `checkInstalledPlugins` iterates every row in installed state without filtering by package type, even though `InstalledEntry` records resource installs as well as plugins. A minimal repro with an installed `resource` entry (`persona/example`) makes `ddx doctor --plugins` emit `/tmp/.../persona.md/package.yaml: open ...: not a directory` and `missing package.yaml` for a non-plugin file. This breaks the plugin audit for users who have installed single resources.
Suggested fix: Restrict `ddx doctor --plugins` to package types that actually represent plugin roots (at least `plugin`, and `workflow` if workflows are intentionally audited), and skip `resource` entries entirely.
title: integration: limit doctor --plugins to plugin installs

acceptance: TP-002 either describes TC-010 as planned work only, or the repository contains the project-scoped HTTP routes /api/projects and /api/projects/:project, the MCP tools ddx_list_projects and ddx_show_project, and corresponding server tests that verify TC-010.1 through TC-010.9
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: docs/helix/03-test/test-plans/TP-002-server-web-ui.md:157
Category: drift
Severity: medium
Description: TC-010 now says the project-scoped routing and MCP registry checks are implemented in `cli/internal/server/server_test.go`, but the same table keeps every TC-010 case in Planned status and the current server/test code still exposes only legacy `/api/...` routes plus MCP tools such as `ddx_list_beads` and `ddx_show_bead`. There are no `/api/projects` routes, no `ddx_list_projects` or `ddx_show_project` tools, and no matching tests. The updated sentence therefore misstates current coverage and makes the governing test plan disagree with the code it references.
Suggested fix: Either change TP-002 to describe TC-010 as planned future coverage, or implement the scoped `/api/projects...` surfaces, MCP registry tools, and matching tests before claiming those checks are already implemented.
title: drift: TP-002 overstates scoped-routing and MCP test coverage

acceptance: The frontend CI job uses Bun tooling and bun.lock for dependency installation and Playwright execution, and .github/workflows/ci.yml no longer depends on cli/internal/server/frontend/package-lock.json or npm commands for the embedded frontend path
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: .github/workflows/ci.yml:156
Category: drift
Severity: medium
Description: The new frontend-e2e job sets up Node, keys its cache from `cli/internal/server/frontend/package-lock.json`, and runs `npm ci`, `npx playwright install`, and `npm run test:e2e`. The active `typescript-bun` concern and TP-002 declare Bun as the frontend package manager and script runner, with `bun.lock` as the authoritative lockfile and `bunx`/`bun run` as the expected commands. Wiring this job to npm cements a second dependency-resolution path in CI that can drift from the declared Bun toolchain and from local/frontend build behavior.
Suggested fix: Switch the job to `oven-sh/setup-bun`, cache/install from `cli/internal/server/frontend/bun.lock`, and run the Playwright install/tests with `bunx` and `bun run` so CI matches the active concern contract.
title: drift: run embedded frontend CI with Bun practices

acceptance: architecture.md and SD-019 define the same fatal-vs-recoverable registry error policy, including explicit duplicate-id behavior, and TP-002 reflects the chosen behavior in planned or implemented coverage
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: docs/helix/02-design/architecture.md:223
Category: integration
Severity: medium
Description: The reviewed docs disagree about how invalid project registries behave. `architecture.md` says a project-registry mismatch should fail only the affected project entry and keep siblings available, explicitly naming duplicate ids as one such case. SD-019 instead says duplicate project ids are a startup/configuration error and that an invalid registry should fail fast before serving partial context. Those are incompatible operator and implementation contracts for one of the key multi-project isolation paths.
Suggested fix: Split registry errors into recoverable per-entry failures versus fatal registry-shape errors, then align architecture.md, SD-019, and any related tests around the chosen policy. Duplicate ids should have one unambiguous outcome.
title: integration: align duplicate-project-id failure policy

acceptance: A regression test covers a mixed log directory where one harness has routing-outcomes.jsonl rows and another harness still has post-cutoff legacy-only sessions.jsonl rows, and ddx agent usage reports both harnesses without double-counting mirrored sessions.
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: cli/cmd/agent_usage.go:247
Category: integration
Severity: medium
Description: The new compatibility merge uses one global `migrationCutoff` taken from the earliest routing outcome in the log directory and then drops every legacy session row at or after that timestamp before merging (`aggregateUsageFromRoutingMetrics` + `aggregateUsageAggs`). That still under-reports mixed stores when routing metrics exist for one harness but another harness still has legacy-only rows after that cutoff. Repro with the current binary: create `routing-outcomes.jsonl` containing only a claude row at `2026-04-09T10:00:00Z`, create `sessions.jsonl` containing that mirrored claude row plus a codex legacy row at `2026-04-10T10:00:00Z`, then run `ddx agent usage --since 2026-04-01 --format json`; the output only includes claude and silently drops codex even though the log directory still contains a valid post-cutoff legacy session.
Suggested fix: Make the compatibility boundary and dedupe key harness-scoped or row-scoped rather than global, so legacy rows without a matching routing outcome remain visible until that harness/session has actually migrated; add regression coverage for a mixed directory where one harness has routing outcomes and another still only has legacy sessions after the first outcome timestamp.
title: integration: keep legacy-only harness rows visible during mixed usage migration

acceptance: Given an installed plugin root contains an invalid package.yaml, when `ddx doctor --plugins` runs, then it reports the manifest validation error without also reporting `missing package.yaml`
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: cli/internal/registry/audit.go:24
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: `AuditInstalledEntry` unconditionally adds `missing package.yaml` whenever `loadPackageDefinitionForAudit` returns `manifestPresent=false`. For malformed manifests, `loadPackageDefinitionForAudit` also returns `false`, so doctor reports both the real validation problem and a bogus missing-file error. Repro: a plugin root with `api_version: [not-a-scalar]` produces both `unsupported api_version "[not-a-scalar]"` and `missing package.yaml`.
Suggested fix: Distinguish `manifest missing` from `manifest invalid` in the audit helper and only append `missing package.yaml` for the `os.IsNotExist` case.
title: correctness: avoid reporting malformed manifests as missing package.yaml

acceptance: Closing a bead with 'ddx bead close <id> --commit <short-sha>' stores the full 40-character commit SHA in bead evidence; ddx bead show ddx-7e88c022 --json reports closing_commit_sha=4563fa77d88cfb0f242925a927e913841b43868c; and the relevant bead close acceptance tests pass.
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: .ddx/beads.jsonl:6
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: Commit fb9bdf1 closes ddx-7e88c022 with closing_commit_sha=4563fa7, an abbreviated SHA. The close store documents this field as the exact closing commit SHA when known, but manual close evidence is persisted verbatim. Replay later resolves closing_commit_sha through git rev-parse to choose the baseline commit, so storing a short SHA weakens audit and replay determinism as the repository grows and abbreviations can become ambiguous or invalid. In the current tracker, this reviewed row is one of only two abbreviated closing_commit_sha values; the durable convention elsewhere is full 40-character SHAs.
Suggested fix: Normalize ddx bead close --commit input through git rev-parse before storing it, repair ddx-7e88c022 to the full 40-character 4563fa77d88cfb0f242925a927e913841b43868c value, and add regression coverage for manual close provenance normalization.
title: correctness: normalize closing_commit_sha to full git SHAs

description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-002-web-stack.md:147
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: The updated ADR now shows the embedded Playwright config launching `${cliRoot}/build/ddx server --port 18080`, but the run instructions still say only `bun run test:e2e`. That command does not build `cli/build/ddx` or refresh the embedded frontend bundle. On a clean checkout, or after frontend changes, following the ADR literally can fail because the binary is missing or stale. CI avoids this by running `bun run build` in `cli/internal/server/frontend` and `make build` in `cli` before `bun run test:e2e`.
Suggested fix: Update ADR-002's Playwright run instructions to state the required build prerequisites explicitly, or change the documented workflow to invoke a command that builds the frontend bundle and CLI binary before running Playwright.
title: correctness: document Playwright build prerequisites

acceptance: A review bead that already carries a valid closing_commit_sha keeps that SHA after ddx bead close when no new close commit lands, while stale prior-HEAD tracker provenance is still removed for metadata-only review closes
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: cli/cmd/bead.go:649
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: Commit f645daf fixes the stale-review-provenance case by deleting any existing `closing_commit_sha` before a review bead is closed without `--commit`, but the new branch cannot distinguish stale provenance from a valid implementation boundary that was intentionally set earlier. Reproducing against the checked-out source with `/tmp/ddx-review` built from `cli/` and `git.auto_commit: never` shows that creating a review bead, setting `closing_commit_sha` to the real HEAD via `bead update --set`, and then running `bead close` removes the field entirely from the closed bead. That regresses FEAT-019 replay/audit semantics for review beads that already carried correct provenance, because the supported close path now erases valid history rather than only suppressing misleading tracker-only backfills.
Suggested fix: Narrow the pre-close cleanup to only stale tracker-only provenance, or preserve an existing `closing_commit_sha` unless the command can prove it points at the prior metadata-only HEAD being repaired; add regression coverage for the preserve-valid-provenance path under metadata-only review close.
title: correctness: preserve valid review closing provenance on metadata-only close

acceptance: AgentRunConfig default preset and .ddx/config.yaml use a supported embedded-agent preset name; no active config/runtime preset path relies on unknown-name fallback; and a regression test proves the default preset is in the embedded library's supported preset set
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:169
Category: integration
Severity: medium
Description: The migration switched the embedded runtime to github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/agent, but the default preset in AgentRunConfig still uses `"forge"`, and the checked-in sample config still sets `.ddx/config.yaml` to `preset: forge`. In v0.1.0, the agent library only exposes presets `agent`, `benchmark`, `minimal`, `claude`, `codex`, and `cursor`; unknown names silently fall back to `agent`. That means DDx now ignores the configured/default preset name with no warning, which makes prompt behavior implicit and leaves the runtime/config surface out of sync with the migrated library.
Suggested fix: Update the embedded default and checked-in config to a supported preset name (`agent` or another intentional choice), remove stale forge preset references from the config surface, and add a regression test that the configured default preset is one of the embedded library's supported presets rather than relying on unknown-name fallback.
title: integration: replace unsupported forge preset defaults after ddx-agent migration

acceptance: With git.auto_commit=always and DDX_BEAD_DIR unset, closing a bead whose landed implementation changes only tracked .ddx/* files still records closing_commit_sha for the landed close commit, and ddx agent replay can use that commit boundary instead of falling back to sessionless/manual mode.
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: cli/cmd/bead.go:80
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: Commit 71f97be suppresses closing_commit_sha whenever the landed close commit touches only paths under .ddx/. That is too broad: .ddx contains real governed artifacts such as config and other tracked workspace files, not just tracker backfills. In a temp repo with git.auto_commit=always and DDX_BEAD_DIR unset, creating a bead, modifying a tracked .ddx/notes.txt file, and running ddx bead close lands a close commit that changes .ddx/beads.jsonl and .ddx/notes.txt, but the bead still omits closing_commit_sha. FEAT-019 only says to omit metadata-only tracker backfills; dropping provenance for legitimate .ddx-scoped work makes replay/audit fall back to sessionless mode even though the implementation boundary is available.
Suggested fix: Detect metadata-only close commits more narrowly, for example by distinguishing pure .ddx/beads.jsonl backfills from other tracked .ddx artifacts, and add regression coverage for a close commit that lands tracked .ddx work.
title: correctness: preserve closing provenance for legitimate .ddx-only work

acceptance: The supervisor state machine validates execution readiness against the same resolved base revision and execution-contract snapshot that govern the ensuing execute-bead attempt
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: docs/helix/02-design/contracts/API-001-execute-bead-supervisor-contract.md:59
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: API-001 chooses a candidate using the generic execution-ready validator before it resolves the base revision and governing execution-contract snapshot. FEAT-006 says execution documents are resolved from the base revision before the agent runs, and those pre-run versions govern the current iteration. If the supervisor validates selection against HEAD or server state first and only later resolves `--from` or checkpoint-derived base state, it can launch a bead against a different execution-definition snapshot than the one it validated.
Suggested fix: Resolve the effective base revision and governing execution-contract snapshot before structural validation/selection, or state explicitly that readiness validation must run against the same base snapshot that will govern the execution attempt.
title: correctness: validate execute-bead readiness against the resolved base snapshot

acceptance: Given an installed plugin entry with an empty type field and plugin-root files, when ddx doctor --plugins runs, then it still audits that entry and reports manifest or structure problems instead of silently skipping it
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: cli/cmd/doctor.go:454
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: `checkInstalledPlugins` now skips any installed entry whose `type` is empty unless the package name exists in `BuiltinRegistry()`. That fixes resource false-positives, but it also suppresses `ddx doctor --plugins` for legacy or third-party plugin installs whose older `installed.yaml` rows lack `type`. The project design explicitly keeps a builtin-registry fallback for legacy packages and expects old packages to remain auditable during the migration period, so this filter drops coverage for a supported compatibility path.
Suggested fix: When `entry.Type` is empty, infer plugin-like roots from the recorded files or installed root path instead of skipping outright, and add a regression test for an untyped installed plugin entry that still produces manifest/structure findings.
title: correctness: preserve doctor audit for legacy untyped plugin installs

acceptance: A regression test covers a routing-outcomes.jsonl row plus its mirrored sessions.jsonl row for the same harness with no native_session_id, trace_id, or span_id, and ddx agent usage reports one session rather than double-counting; cd cli && go test ./cmd -run TestAgentUsage -count=1 passes.
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: cli/cmd/agent_usage.go:201
Category: integration
Severity: medium
Description: Commit 5349a98 removes the per-harness cutoff and now suppresses legacy sessions.jsonl rows only when usageMirrorKey finds a matching native_session_id, trace_id, or span_id (aggregateUsageAggs lines 201-207 and aggregateUsageFromRoutingMetrics lines 246-270). Current external-harness writes do not guarantee any of those identifiers: both the session row and routing outcome copy result.AgentSessionID, then only fill correlation IDs when present (cli/internal/agent/runner.go:722-753, cli/internal/agent/routing_metrics.go:231-256). For normal codex/claude-style runs without correlation metadata, both records remain unkeyed, so ddx agent usage now counts the same live run twice. Reproduced with the shipped CLI by creating one codex sessions.jsonl row and one matching routing-outcomes.jsonl row with identical tokens/cost but no mirror IDs; ddx agent usage --since 2026-04-01 --format json reported sessions=2, input_tokens=400, output_tokens=40, cost_usd=5 instead of one session.
Suggested fix: Preserve a compatibility boundary for unkeyed current rows, or emit a stable mirror identifier for every routing outcome/session pair before relying solely on identity-match dedupe; add regression coverage for a mixed current-format session/outcome pair that lacks native_session_id, trace_id, and span_id.
title: integration: avoid double-counting current sessions without mirror IDs in agent usage

acceptance: ddx bead show hx-ddx-agent-target-verify --json reports closing_commit_sha=2b69dbbf9127e98d694a4bd7d07e916458c50e8e, and regression coverage proves updating or closing hx-9c1a1558 leaves unrelated bead fields unchanged
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: .ddx/beads.jsonl:178
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: Commit e61d84c36aa74448ecadb2ad9084767cd453bfe0 correctly finalizes review bead hx-9c1a1558, but the same tracker diff also rewrites unrelated closed bead hx-ddx-agent-target-verify and drops its existing closing_commit_sha=2b69dbbf9127e98d694a4bd7d07e916458c50e8e. FEAT-004 requires unknown fields to be preserved on read/write operations, and FEAT-019 uses closing_commit_sha as durable replay/audit provenance. Losing that field on an unrelated closed bead silently corrupts the replay baseline for the SD-018 verification task even though this commit did not govern that bead. Suggested fix: repair hx-ddx-agent-target-verify so its closing_commit_sha is restored to 2b69dbbf9127e98d694a4bd7d07e916458c50e8e, and add/adjust tracker update coverage so finalizing one bead cannot clobber preserved fields on unrelated rows.
title: correctness: preserve unrelated closing provenance when finalizing review beads

acceptance: In a non-git working directory, 'ddx bead close <id> --commit 1234567890abcdef1234567890abcdef12345678' succeeds and 'ddx bead show <id> --json' reports that exact closing_commit_sha; in a git repo, short commit refs passed to '--commit' are still normalized to full 40-character SHAs; and the relevant bead close acceptance tests pass.
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: cli/cmd/bead.go:567
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: Commit 1bc26181bae1e88cd6c29fc11f20c5904ed2ae8a normalizes `ddx bead close --commit` through `git rev-parse` before persisting the value. That fixes short SHAs inside a git repo, but it also makes manual close provenance unusable in non-git workspaces where bead commands otherwise still work. Reproduced with the built CLI in a temp directory outside git: `ddx bead create ...` succeeds, then `ddx bead close <id> --commit 1234567890abcdef1234567890abcdef12345678` now fails with `git rev-parse ... exit status 128`. Before this change, the command accepted an exact commit SHA verbatim. That is a behavior regression for portable/manual tracker workflows and contradicts the command contract that `--commit` records the exact closing commit SHA when known.
Suggested fix: Normalize only when the supplied value is an abbreviated ref in a git repository, but preserve already-full commit SHAs verbatim when git resolution is unavailable; add regression coverage for `bead close --commit <40-char-sha>` in a non-git workspace.
title: correctness: preserve manual close provenance in non-git workspaces

acceptance: A correlated session row with model=gpt-5.4 and cost_usd=0 is reported with cost_state=known and cost_usd=0 in both Cost and Summary outputs, cost_usd=-1 remains unknown, and cd cli && go test ./internal/processmetrics -count=1 passes
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: cli/internal/processmetrics/processmetrics.go:1084
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: Commit 58d6229309445ce3e571b85e14f1ba423b2198b1 still treats an explicit session row with cost_usd=0 as estimated whenever the model has non-zero pricing. Both sessionCostState and sessionDerivedCost continue to special-case only sess.CostUSD > 0, so a priced-model row such as model=gpt-5.4 with cost_usd=0 falls through to sessionEstimatedCost and is reported as cost_state=estimated with a non-zero derived cost instead of preserving the recorded zero. The new regression test only covers qwen/qwen3-coder-30b, whose estimator also returns 0, so it misses the case that actually matters for explicit zero-cost rows on priced models.
Suggested fix: Treat any explicit cost_usd >= 0 as the authoritative known cost before consulting estimator fallback, and add regression coverage using a priced model plus the -1 sentinel so both known-zero and unknown paths are locked down.
title: correctness: explicit zero-cost rows must override estimator output

Pass 1 correctness: API-001 duplicates post-run and land/preserve ownership around Unified interface for dispatching work to AI coding agents.

Supports multiple harnesses (codex, claude, gemini, etc.) with output capture,
token tracking, session logging, and multi-agent quorum.

Examples:
  ddx agent run --harness codex --prompt task.md
  ddx agent run --quorum majority --harnesses codex,claude --prompt task.md
  ddx agent list
  ddx agent capabilities codex
  ddx agent doctor
  ddx agent log

Usage:
  ddx agent [flags]
  ddx agent [command]

Available Commands:
  benchmark    Run a benchmark suite comparing agent harnesses
  capabilities Show agent model and reasoning-level capabilities
  condense     Filter raw agent output to progress-relevant lines
  doctor       Check agent harness health
  list         List available agent harnesses
  log          Show agent session history
  replay       Replay a bead with a different agent harness or model
  run          Invoke an agent with a prompt
  usage        Show per-harness token and cost summary

Flags:
  -h, --help   help for agent

Global Flags:
      --config string              config file (default is $HOME/.ddx.yml)
      --library-base-path string   override path for DDx library location
  -v, --verbose                    verbose output

Use "ddx agent [command] --help" for more information about a command., and it validates candidate readiness before resolving the governing base snapshot that FEAT-006 says must control execution-document evaluation.

description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: .ddx/beads.jsonl:22
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: During this review, `ddx bead close hx-01c0012d` closed a metadata-only review bead without creating a new git commit, yet the resulting tracker row recorded `closing_commit_sha=ff9a6ce2f6db77c670adcf2874b3f86278dba4e8`. That SHA is the previously reviewed hx-registry-lookpath provenance-repair commit, not the review-bead lifecycle change itself. FEAT-019 explicitly says tracker-only backfills must omit `closing_commit_sha` instead of pointing replay at an unrelated metadata or earlier implementation commit, so the underlying close-path behavior still lets review/repair beads persist misleading replay provenance even after ff9a6ce repaired one specific bead row.
Suggested fix: Make `ddx bead close` omit `closing_commit_sha` when no new close commit is actually created or when the operation is metadata-only tracker maintenance, add regression coverage for that path, and repair hx-01c0012d so its closing provenance is absent rather than pointing at ff9a6ce.
title: correctness: omit misleading closing provenance for metadata-only review closes

Review area: server and UI activity surfaces. Evidence covers FEAT-002, FEAT-008, and the new TD-006 ownership model for external vs embedded agent detail.
</context-digest>
Align DDx server and UI surfaces with the new invocation-activity model.

- Update API/detail semantics for agent activity
- Surface native session and trace references where available
- Keep embedded ddx-agent detail richer when DDx owns or can reference it
- Avoid promising full external provider transcript browsing

acceptance: Codex and Claude provider adapters exist with explicit freshness/source metadata; Codex current quota/headroom comes from native logs, Claude historical usage comes from stats-cache, and Claude current quota is surfaced as unknown unless the spike identifies a stable non-PTY source or an async live-probe snapshot path
description:
<context-digest>
Review area: provider-native adapters. Evidence covers Codex native session JSONL, Claude stats-cache, and the requirement to avoid PTY as the default quota path.
</context-digest>
Implement provider-native adapters that feed DDx routing and reporting.

## Scope
- Codex: native session JSONL adapter for current quota/headroom and recent usage totals
- Claude: stats-cache adapter for historical usage
- Claude: current-quota source implemented only if the spike finds a stable non-PTY path; otherwise explicit unknown semantics or an async live-probe snapshot source
- Common freshness/source metadata for all adapter outputs
notes: Superseded by ddx-f515cd62. Closed because this bead was created with a manually invented hx-* ID instead of a ddx-managed bead ID.
title: Implement provider-native routing signal adapters

acceptance: A written signal-source matrix exists for codex, claude, and embedded ddx-agent covering current quota/headroom, historical usage, freshness, scope, and whether each source is stable enough for preflight routing; any required PTY fallback is specified as an async snapshot source rather than inline routing behavior
description:
<context-digest>
Review area: provider-native routing signals. Evidence covers local Codex session JSONL and state stores, Claude stats/runtime artifacts, and embedded ddx-agent telemetry ownership.
</context-digest>
Spike the provider-native signal sources DDx can use for preflight routing.

## Goals
1. Confirm Codex native session JSONL parsing contract for current rate-limit headroom and recent usage totals
2. Investigate Claude current-quota sources, explicitly preferring non-PTY options such as statusline/SDK/hook/runtime artifacts before any PTY fallback
3. Classify each source by freshness, stability, account-vs-session scope, and routing suitability
4. Document explicit `unknown` semantics where no trustworthy live source exists
5. If PTY fallback is required, define the async snapshot/state model it must feed
notes: Superseded by ddx-7e88c022. Closed because this bead was created with a manually invented hx-* ID instead of a ddx-managed bead ID.
title: Spike provider-native routing signal sources for codex, claude, and ddx-agent

acceptance: DDx stores only the approved minimal routing metrics/activity state, embedded ddx-agent telemetry is referenced rather than duplicated, async quota snapshot history is persisted for live-probe sources, and tests prove external provider transcripts are not required for routing metrics
description:
<context-digest>
Review area: DDx-owned routing metrics implementation. Evidence covers the metrics spike outcome, TD-006 activity semantics, and the embedded ddx-agent telemetry boundary.
</context-digest>
Implement the minimal DDx-owned routing metrics/activity layer.

## Scope
- Persist only routing-relevant metrics and invocation metadata
- Store native session and trace references where available
- Persist async quota snapshot history for any live-probe quota source
- Compute the inputs needed for relative subscription-burn estimation
- Do not duplicate external provider prompt/response bodies
- Preserve backward compatibility for existing activity/session rows
notes: Superseded by ddx-944a86ab. Closed because this bead was created with a manually invented hx-* ID instead of a ddx-managed bead ID.
title: Implement minimal DDx-owned routing metrics, snapshots, and activity records

acceptance: A minimal DDx-owned routing-metrics schema is defined with fields, freshness/retention policy, async quota snapshot history, subscription-burn estimation inputs, and explicit exclusions proving DDx does not need to duplicate provider transcripts or session logs
description:
<context-digest>
Review area: minimal DDx-owned observability. Evidence covers FEAT-006 invocation activity ownership, SD-015 routing signals, and the embedded ddx-agent telemetry boundary.
</context-digest>
Define the smallest DDx-owned state needed to rank harnesses on performance and reliability.

## Goals
1. Specify fields such as recent success/failure, recent latency, and last observed token/cost values when available
2. Define freshness and retention rules for routing metrics
3. Define async quota snapshot history for any live-probe source and how snapshots map to native usage accumulation over time
4. Define a relative subscription-burn estimate that can balance providers without direct billing APIs
5. Define how embedded ddx-agent session logging and OTEL are referenced without duplication
notes: Superseded by ddx-a62bc00d. Closed because this bead was created with a manually invented hx-* ID instead of a ddx-managed bead ID.
title: Spike minimal DDx-owned routing metrics, snapshots, and burn estimation

acceptance: Routing, `ddx agent doctor --routing`, and `ddx agent usage` all consume the normalized routing signal model; quota blocked candidates are rejected, unknown quota remains viable with reduced confidence, and selection explanations include source freshness, subscription-burn inputs, and the freshest cached snapshot when live probing is involved
description:
<context-digest>
Review area: normalized routing integration. Evidence covers SD-015 candidate planning, FEAT-014 signal semantics, and FEAT-006 doctor/usage surfaces.
</context-digest>
Integrate normalized routing signals into selection and operator-facing surfaces.

## Scope
- Feed routing candidate planning from normalized signals
- Update doctor routing output with source and freshness details
- Rework `ddx agent usage` to consume adapters/metrics instead of a duplicate session ledger
- Use quota snapshot history and relative subscription-burn estimation as balancing inputs where applicable
- Consume the freshest cached quota snapshot rather than inline PTY scraping when live probing is required
- Add routing tests for quota blocked vs unknown behavior
notes: Superseded by ddx-f73cbe0d. Closed because this bead was created with a manually invented hx-* ID instead of a ddx-managed bead ID.
title: Integrate normalized routing signals into selection, doctor, and usage

acceptance: DefaultTimeoutMS in types.go matches the timeout_ms value documented in FEAT-006-agent-service.md
description:
Code defines DefaultTimeoutMS = 600000 (10 minutes) but FEAT-006 spec says timeout_ms: 300000 (5 minute default).

## Decision required
Either:
1. Update code to 300000ms (5 min) to match spec, OR
2. Update FEAT-006 spec to 600000ms (10 min) if that's intentional
title: Reconcile DefaultTimeoutMS with FEAT-006 spec

acceptance: ddx bead show hx-1619d2ac --json omits closing_commit_sha
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: .ddx/beads.jsonl:50
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: Commit 7e1f93392ee5a6138e91b64efd777ccbc47e78ee changes hx-1619d2ac.closing_commit_sha from a1606778834e7b40b883a0859b00851c780e9337 to 1169d2c3c59b784d01ce765ebba1ec091498fc4e, but both SHAs are tracker-only commits that touch only .ddx/beads.jsonl. FEAT-019 says tracker-only backfills must omit closing_commit_sha so replay falls back to the sessionless/manual baseline instead of pretending a metadata-only commit is the governed implementation boundary. The reviewed commit therefore replaces one misleading provenance value with another and leaves the closed review-finding bead hx-1619d2ac advertising a replay boundary it should not have.
Suggested fix: Clear hx-1619d2ac.closing_commit_sha through the supported ddx bead update path so the closed review-finding bead no longer records tracker-only provenance; keep the broader metadata-only review-finding close behavior aligned with FEAT-019 separately.
title: correctness: clear tracker-only closing provenance on hx-1619d2ac

Category: correctness

title: correctness: omit misleading closing provenance on hx-ffb390a4

acceptance: ddx bead show hx-2ee85fc3 --json reports closing_commit_sha=54dec9cd29a31f161332e8f3215e5f04033f6eef, the stored value is a full 40-character SHA rather than 54dec9c, and replay/audit provenance for the FEAT-016 execution bead no longer depends on downstream ref expansion
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: .ddx/beads.jsonl:64
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: Commit 843e602 closes hx-2ee85fc3 with closing_commit_sha=54dec9c, an abbreviated ref, even though the current close/update contract normalizes git-backed closing provenance to the full 40-character SHA. FEAT-019 uses closing_commit_sha as replay/audit provenance, and the acceptance tests in cli/cmd/bead_acceptance_test.go assert that short refs are expanded before they are stored. Leaving the bead closed with an abbreviated value makes this row inconsistent with the supported provenance format and forces downstream consumers to re-resolve an inexact identifier.
Suggested fix: Repair hx-2ee85fc3 through ddx bead update so closing_commit_sha stores 54dec9cd29a31f161332e8f3215e5f04033f6eef, and ensure the close workflow that produced 843e602 records the normalized full SHA rather than the short ref.
title: correctness: normalize hx-2ee85fc3 closing commit provenance

acceptance: A regression test models the production write order by placing the mirrored unkeyed session row before the routing outcome row and ddx agent usage still reports one session for that run; cd cli && go test ./cmd -run TestAgentUsage -count=1 passes.
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: cli/cmd/agent_usage.go:208
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: The new cutoff-based dedupe only skips session rows whose timestamp is at or after the first routing outcome for a harness, but current runners write the session row before they append the routing outcome (cli/internal/agent/runner.go:169-170 and cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:162-163). For unkeyed runs without native_session_id, trace_id, or span_id, the mirrored session entry therefore remains just before the cutoff and is still counted, so ddx agent usage can continue double-counting the exact current-run duplicates this commit intended to remove. The new regression test encodes the opposite timestamp order (session after outcome), so it passes without exercising the real runtime path.
Suggested fix: Deduplicate unkeyed mirrored rows using a criterion that matches production write order, or emit a stable mirror identifier on every current run and key dedupe on that identifier; update the regression test so the session timestamp precedes the routing outcome timestamp as the runners do in production.
title: correctness: unkeyed session dedupe misses real log write order

acceptance: The execute-bead contract explicitly documents the supervisor-visible result fields and state enum, and API-001 only depends on that documented surface rather than inferred reason strings
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: docs/helix/02-design/contracts/API-001-execute-bead-supervisor-contract.md:69
Category: drift
Severity: medium
Description: The revised contract now says the supervisor captures the result state reported by `ddx agent execute-bead` and classifies outcomes such as execution failure, post-run check failure, land conflict, and success. The current command surface does not publish that enum anywhere: `ExecuteBeadResult` in `cli/cmd/agent_execute_bead.go:18-36` exposes only `outcome` (`merged|preserved|no-changes|error`) plus a free-form `reason`, and FEAT-006 documents only landed/preserved iteration outcomes in the commit summary. A supervisor built from API-001 would have to reverse-engineer undocumented meanings from ad hoc strings, which is too brittle for a contract-level dependency.
Suggested fix: Add a documented, stable execute-bead result schema that includes the supervisor-facing state categories API-001 needs, or narrow API-001 so it only relies on the result fields that `execute-bead` actually guarantees today.
title: drift: define the execute-bead result surface API-001 expects

acceptance: SD-015 retention rules keep routing outcome samples within the same model-level attribution group used elsewhere in the design, so different canonical targets or exact model pins on one surface do not share one rolling sample window; and the retention wording is consistent with FEAT-006 and FEAT-014 about model-specific routing signals.
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-015-agent-routing-and-catalog-resolution.md:230
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: The fix adds `canonical_target` to `RoutingOutcome`, `QuotaSnapshot`, and `BurnSummary`, and earlier in SD-015 defines `CanonicalTarget` as the stable attribution key for downstream routing metrics. But the retention rule still says outcome samples are kept "per harness surface". That would still mix and evict samples across different models on the same surface, undermining the model-specific separation the rest of the design now requires.
Suggested fix: Update the freshness/retention section so outcome-sample retention is scoped by the same model-level attribution key (`canonical_target`, or the exact equivalent attribution key) rather than only harness/surface.
title: correctness: scope routing-outcome retention by canonical target

description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: .ddx/beads.jsonl:8
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: Commit 8eaf934b6d106b9f513fb76bb10f2091e67b87d5 improves ddx-83777abd by repointing its closing provenance at the actual implementation commit, but it stores only the 7-character prefix `d84aa2b` instead of the exact commit SHA. The tracker close contract documents `closing_commit_sha` as the exact closing commit SHA when known, and replay/audit use this field as durable provenance. The abbreviated prefix resolves today only because it is currently unique; as the repository grows it can become ambiguous or invalid, which would break later replay or audit runs for this bead.
Suggested fix: Rewrite ddx-83777abd.closing_commit_sha to the full 40-character commit SHA `d84aa2b457f234c07a8195346b09fd416f13d478`, and keep manual close/provenance normalization aligned on full object IDs for durable replay semantics.
title: correctness: store full closing_commit_sha for ddx-83777abd

acceptance: FEAT-018 and SD-018 document the nested skill.* SKILL.md schema as the stable contract for bundled DDx skills, with no migration required for the shipped skills.
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-018-plugin-api-stability.md:116
Category: correctness
Severity: high
Description: SD-018 defines stable SKILL.md frontmatter as top-level `name` and `description`, and its validation model requires those keys. Existing bundled DDx skills still use the legacy nested `skill.name` / `skill.description` schema documented in SD-011 and present in cli/internal/skills/ddx-bead/SKILL.md. If install/doctor validation is implemented from SD-018 as written, built-in DDx skills will fail validation or require an undocumented breaking migration, which violates FEAT-018's backward-compatibility goal.
Suggested fix: Update SD-018 to either adopt the existing nested `skill.*` schema as the stable contract or explicitly define a backward-compatible migration/validation rule that accepts both legacy and stable forms, with tests covering bundled DDx skills.
title: correctness: reconcile SD-018 skill schema with existing DDx skills

acceptance: ddx bead show hx-3fafe2df --json omits closing_commit_sha, and cd cli && go test ./cmd -run TestBeadCommandsCloseOmitsMetadataOnlyReviewFindingClosingCommitSha -count=1 passes
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: .ddx/beads.jsonl:77
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: Commit a043cfa52fc867964fc63062d26e510a090babfb closes review-finding bead hx-3fafe2df with closing_commit_sha=25786b96c7e27a478d5982f5776c7e9c89308b2f. The referenced commit is itself a tracker-only provenance repair that changes only .ddx/beads.jsonl, so FEAT-019 replay should not treat it as a governed implementation boundary. The close-path suppression in cli/cmd/bead.go only classifies labels action:review, kind:review, and phase:review as metadata-only review work, which lets phase:build review-finding beads record misleading replay provenance even when their implementation is a pure tracker backfill.
Suggested fix: Clear hx-3fafe2df.closing_commit_sha and broaden the close-path metadata-only review classification and regression coverage so tracker-only review-finding beads omit closing provenance by default.
title: correctness: omit closing provenance on tracker-only review-finding closes

acceptance: The contract states an explicit queue-selection rule consistent with SD-004 ready ordering and HELIX eligibility ownership, and the generic execution validator is used only to validate structural executability rather than choose policy priority
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: docs/helix/02-design/contracts/API-001-execute-bead-supervisor-contract.md:59
Category: drift
Severity: medium
Description: API-001 says the supervisor should read the ready bead set and choose the best candidate using the generic execution-ready validator. But the same contract says HELIX owns which beads are eligible for the loop, and SD-004 already defines deterministic ready ordering as priority then stable snapshot order. The generic DDx validator can say whether one bead is structurally executable; it does not define queue priority or workflow policy. Leaving candidate choice as 'best' via the validator either invents hidden DDx scheduling policy or makes the first worker's queue behavior undefined.
Suggested fix: Separate queue ordering from readiness validation. Specify whether the supervisor consumes a pre-ordered execution-ready queue (for example SD-004 ordering plus workflow eligibility filters) and then uses the validator only as a gate on the selected candidate.
title: drift: define supervisor queue selection independently from the generic validator

Epic to track the migration from forge (v0.0.4) to ddx-agent and address outstanding agent-related improvements.

## Background
forge has been renamed to ddx-agent and published a new release. The DDx CLI still depends on the old forge package and needs migration.

## Scope

- Import path updates across cli/internal/agent/
- Config/runtime naming cleanup (preset, comments, tests)
- Address blocked agent-related beads
- Documentation cleanup
notes: Closed during 2026-04-10 queue organization review. The migration epic has no open child beads left, active code/config/docs no longer use live forge naming, and the remaining historical references are in review artifacts or negative tests rather than active runtime/config surfaces.
title: EPIC: Migrate forge to ddx-agent and implement agent improvements

acceptance: execute-bead rebases the worktree result onto current HEAD before attempting ff-merge; when the rebase succeeds the result lands without a merge commit; when the rebase conflicts the iteration is preserved under the hidden ref and the target branch remains unchanged; and tests cover the case where the target branch advanced during execution
description:
FEAT-006 step 10 specifies 'land by rebase + fast-forward semantics' but the implementation only attempts `git merge --ff-only`.

## Current behavior
If the target branch has advanced since the base revision, the ff-merge fails and the result is preserved under a hidden ref instead of being rebased and landed.

## Fix required
Before attempting ff-merge, rebase the worktree commits onto the current branch HEAD. If the rebase succeeds, then ff-merge and keep linear history. If the rebase fails because of conflicts, preserve the iteration under the hidden ref, leave the target branch untouched, and report the non-landed result deterministically.
title: Implement rebase + ff semantics for execute-bead land flow

acceptance: After execute-bead completes, dirty worktree changes are either restored via git stash pop or the user is explicitly warned with the stash ref so they can restore manually
description:
execute-bead stashes dirty worktree changes but never restores them after execution.

## Current behavior
When execute-bead detects a dirty worktree, it calls git stash to checkpoint changes but never calls git stash pop. User's in-progress work remains silently stashed.

## Fix required
Add git stash pop (or git stash apply) in the defer cleanup after the worktree is removed, or document that stash is intentional and warn the user with the stash ref.
title: Fix execute-bead dirty worktree stash/restore bug

acceptance: Given a plugin package has a valid package.yaml but a declared skill directory missing SKILL.md or required frontmatter, when `ddx install --local` or registry install runs, then the command exits non-zero, writes no installed.yaml entry, and surfaces the same structural validation error that doctor would report
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: cli/cmd/install.go:212
Category: correctness
Severity: high
Description: The new install paths only validate `package.yaml`, then proceed to symlink/copy skills without reusing the structural checks that doctor performs. `installLocal` calls `LoadPackageManifest` and then links skills, while `InstallPackage` does the same for tarball installs. As a result, malformed plugins still install successfully. Repro: a local plugin with a valid package.yaml and a declared skill directory containing `bad-skill/` but no `SKILL.md` installs successfully instead of failing fast.
Suggested fix: Share one structural validator between install and doctor so install rejects missing `SKILL.md`, invalid frontmatter, and related contract violations before writing install state.
title: correctness: fail install when plugin skill metadata is invalid
